text: The laparoscopic view reveals significant sigmoid diverticulitis with pericolonic inflammatory adhesions and a small interloop abscess. The surgeon drains the intra-abdominal purulent fluid and irrigates the abdomen with an antibiotic solution, addressing the microperforation of the sigmoid colon without gross fecal contamination.
The surgeon performs a primary repair of the perforated diverticulitis using multiple intracorporeal sutures with a two-hole etabond technique, aiming to avoid the need for a temporary colostomy.
